title: A Fugitive Goat Jumped Off a Sheriff's SUV in Ohio and Vanished
script: A goat just outsmarted Ohio sheriffs! On June 11, 8 goats were spotted trespassing in a neighbor's backyard on Oxford Middletown Road in Butler County, Ohio. Deputies rushed in with snacks, tried every trick they knew, and successfully wrangled 7 of them. But 1 goat — described as a white male with brown face markings, and officially wanted on counts of extreme stubbornness — jumped off the roof of a sheriff's SUV and sprinted into the woods. The Butler County Sheriff's Office says the fugitive is short, highly mischievous, and still at large. They're asking anyone in the area to call the sheriff if they spot him. After leaping off a police SUV, how do you catch a goat that doesn't want to be caught?
